heroHeadline: September 11, 2001: Al-Qaeda Attacks the United States
heroSubtitle: Four hijacked aircraft killed 2,977 people across New York, Virginia, and Pennsylvania. The deadliest terrorist attack in history sparked two decades of war, transformed global security, and left over 100,000 first responders with ongoing health consequences.
footerNote: Data sourced from 9/11 Commission Report, NIST investigations, FBI/CIA declassified documents, Congressional records, DoD, FDNY, NYPD, NYC OCME, NIOSH WTC Health Program, September 11th VCF, and major news outlets.
